records.stage_evidence: Clinical history states 'RT DJ STENT' and findings confirm 'Well-positioned Right double-J ureteral stent'.

ocr.verbatim:
PATIENT NAME : 
PROCEDURE DATE : 02/04/2026
AGE/SEX : 29 YEAR/MALE
REFERRING DR. :

X-RAY KUBS

CLINICAL HISTORY : RT DJ STENT

FIDINGS:
Visualized renal outlines are normal in size, shape,position and axis.
Well-positioned Right double-J ureteral stent in the Right flank.
No radio-opaque calcutication is seen in renal areas, hepatobiliary region or pancreatic area.
Both psoas shadows are normal and bilaterally symmetrical.
No abnormal soft tissue mass seen.
Lumbosacral spine is normal.

IMPRESSION:
NO RADIO-O PAQUE RENAL STONES OR GALL STONES SEEN.
Well-positioned Right double-J ureteral stent in the Right flank.

Please correlate clinically.

• Timing : Monday to Sunday 08:00 AM to 08:00 PM
• इमरजेंसी सेवा 24 घण्टे उपलब्ध है।
• Not For Medico Legal Purpose

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an X-ray KUB (Kidney, Ureter, Bladder) performed on 02/04/2026. It confirms the presence of a well-positioned right double-J ureteral stent and rules out radio-opaque renal or gall stones. The report also notes normal renal outlines, psoas shadows, and lumbosacral spine.
key_observations: Procedure date: 02/04/2026
Patient age/sex: 29-year-old male
Findings: Well-positioned right double-J ureteral stent in the right flank
Imression: No radio-opaque renal stones or gall stones seen
Note: 'Please correlate clinically' and 'Not For Medico Legal Purpose'

records.summary: A post-procedure KUB X-ray showing a ureteral catheter in the pelvis, with the patient's spine and pelvic bones visible. The image is dated April 2, 2026.
key_observations: A ureteral catheter is visible extending from the upper abdomen into the pelvis.
Spine and pelvic bones are clearly visible.
Image is labeled with patient age (29 Y/M) and date (02-04-2026).

records.stage_evidence: Report describes pre-contrast findings (plain KUB) and post-contrast IVP findings

ocr.verbatim:
NAME OF PATIENT :

AGE/ SEX: 29/Year/male
REFERRED BY: DR.
DATE: 22-03-2026
INVESTIGATION: IVP

IVP

PLAIN X-RAY KUB:-
A.R.O. shadow of size 26X15mm is seen on Right side of abdomen.
B/L psoas shadows are normal.

IVP:-
Right kidney &Ureter:-
There are good excretory functions, Nephrogram and pyelogram phases are normal,

PCS are dilated. R.O. shadow is seen area of PUJ.
Right ureter is normal in caliber.

Left kidney &Ureter:-
There are delayed excretory functions. There is increasingly dense nephrogram and
pyelogram phases.
Left ureter is not opacified.

Urinary bladder:-
UB is dilated with smooth walls. No filling defect seen .
Post void urine volume is insignificant.

IMP:-
Right side calculus with delayed renal excretory function.
Normal renal excretory function on right side.

Timing : Monday to Sunday 08:00 AM to 08:00 PM
• इमर्जेंसी सेवा 24 घण्टे उपलब्ध है।
• Not For Medico Legal Purpose

records.summary: This is a typed IVP report for a 29-year-old male patient. The report details findings from a plain KUB X-ray and an IVP study, noting a right-sided calculus with delayed renal excretory function, while the right kidney and ureter show normal function.
key_observations: Patient is 29-year-old male
Plain KUB shows a 26x15mm A.R.O. shadow on the right side of the abdomen
Right kidney and ureter show normal excretory function and normal nephrogram/pyelogram phases
Left kidney shows delayed excretory function with increasingly dense nephrogram/pyelogram phases and non-opacified ureter
Urinary bladder is dilated with smooth walls and no filling defect
Final impression: Right side calculus with delayed renal excretory function; normal function on the right side
Report includes a disclaimer: 'Not For Medico Legal Purpose'

ocr.verbatim:
PATENT NAME:-
AGE:- 29 SEX:- MALE
REF. BY DR.:- SELF.
DATE:- 18-03-2026
ULTRASOUND WHOLE ABDOMEN
NO EVIDENCE OF ASCITIS,PARAORTIC LYMPHNODES&PLEURAL EFFUSION.
* LIVER
Liver is normal in shape, size &echo pattern. No mass lesion is seen.
Intrahepaticbiliary channels are of normal caliber.
Echo texture& echo pattern of liver is normal.
* GB
GB is normal in shape, size & contour. No mass or calculus is seen.
CBD is of normal caliber.
* PANCREAS
Pancreas is normal in shape, size &echopattern. No mass lesion is seen.
Pancreatic duct is normal.
* SPLEEN
Spleen is normal in shape, size &echopattern. Splenic vein is normal in size.
* RIGHT KIDNEY
Rt kidney is normal in shape, size &ech.opattern. No mass lesion is seen. Multiple
Echogenic foci of largest size measuring about 26X16mm seen in upper and mid Pole of Right
Kidney.Pelvicalyceal system is normal.Cortico-medullary differentiation is intact.
* LEFT KIDNEY
Lt kidney is normal in shape, size &ech.opattern.No mass lesion or calculus is seen. Pelvicalyceal
system is normal.Cortico-medullary differentiation is intact.
* UB
UB is normal in shape, size & wall thickness.
No mass or calculus is seen.
* PROSTATE
Prostate is normal is shape, size &echopattern. No mass lesion is seen.
Capsule is intact. No evidence of Calcification.
Both seminal vesicles are normal in shape, size &echopattern.
IMPRESSION:-
RIGHT RENAL CALCULI.
*This is the provisional Diagnosis please co-relate with your clinical findings & futher investigaions. Not
for Medico
legal use

records.summary: This is a typed ultrasound report of the whole abdomen for a 29-year-old male patient, dated 18-03-2026. The report details normal findings for the liver, gallbladder, pancreas, spleen, and both kidneys, except for the right kidney which shows multiple echogenic foci, leading to an impression of right renal calculi.
key_observations: Patient is 29-year-old male.
Ultrasound of whole abdomen performed on 18-03-2026.
Normal findings for liver, gallbladder, pancreas, spleen, and left kidney.
Right kidney shows multiple echogenic foci (largest 26x16mm) in upper and mid pole.
Final impression: Right renal calculi.
Report notes provisional diagnosis and advises correlation with clinical findings.

records.summary: Four sequential IVP images showing the pelvicalyceal system at different time points after contrast injection: plain, 5 minutes, 30 minutes, and full bladder phase.
key_observations: Four images arranged in a 2x2 grid, all labeled 'Chest PA' with 'R' indicating right side.
Time points labeled: 'PLAIN', '5 MIN', '30 MIN', 'FULL BLADDER'.
Contrast material is visible in the renal collecting systems and urinary bladder, particularly in the 30-minute and full bladder images.
Image quality is fair with some graininess and low contrast, but anatomical structures are discernible.

limitations: Low contrast and grainy image quality
Partial view of the pelvis in some frames
Images appear to be printed on a lightbox or film viewer
